text:
There once was a wise old man who lived in a tiny village near a great ocean. He loved swimming in those waters.

One day a giant bird came soaring over the ocean. The old man watched it fly gracefully. He wondered how such a magnificent creature lived.

That evening, he decided to find out. He chased the bird down onto land.

The bird pecked fiercely. The old man felt pain.

"No fair!" he cried.

"The circle of life," the bird squawked.

That night under the pale moonlight, he died.

Now, every winter when owls fly south, old feathers like those fall from that bird.

Every autumn, someone finds them and puts them in a hat. People love that hat.

That old man didn't have a hat. Perhaps he would have liked the owl feathers:

That thought made him smile. Even an old man likes a good story.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
